task_completion.description: Verify that a Firefox bookmark folder named 'World Bank Research' was created with at least four bookmarks from worldbank.org.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Firefox Library window, Bookmarks menu, or sidebar showing a folder named 'World Bank Research' containing at least 4 saved pages from worldbank.org or data.worldbank.org.

task_completion.description: Verify that a file named 'world_bank_africa_report.json' is saved in the '~/Documents/' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, file manager, or terminal showing the file 'world_bank_africa_report.json' located in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Verify that the JSON file contains the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the JSON file for a key-value pair setting the title to exactly 'Sub-Saharan Africa Comparative Economic Briefing'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the JSON file includes data for Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa, along with the data year.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the JSON structure to ensure Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa are present, and there is a field indicating the year of the data (e.g., 2022 or 2023) for each.

task_completion.description: Verify that the JSON file contains the five required economic indicators for each country.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for keys representing GDP, GDP per capita, life expectancy, population, and access to electricity for all three countries.

task_completion.description: Verify that GDP is in billions, population is in millions, and both are rounded to one decimal place.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the numerical values for GDP and population in the JSON file. They should be scaled appropriately (e.g., ~477.4 for Nigeria's GDP, ~223.2 for its population) and have exactly one decimal place.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually used the web browser to access the World Bank Open Data portal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing Firefox open and navigated to data.worldbank.org, rather than the agent using terminal commands like curl or wget to fetch data.

integrity.description: Verify that the data values in the JSON file are genuine and not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the values in the JSON file fall within the plausible ranges provided in the privileged info (e.g., Nigeria GDP between 250 and 650, South Africa population between 55 and 68).

integrity.description: Verify that the output file is formatted as valid JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the text editor or terminal output to ensure the file uses proper JSON syntax (curly braces, double quotes for keys, colons, commas).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Plausible ranges for 2022/2023 data: NIGERIA: GDP [250, 650] billion USD, GDP per capita [1000, 3500] USD, life expectancy [48, 60] years, population [190, 250] million, electricity access [45, 80]%. KENYA: GDP [70, 160] billion USD, GDP per capita [1500, 3000] USD, life expectancy [55, 70] years, population [45, 65] million, electricity access [60, 90]%. SOUTH AFRICA: GDP [300, 500] billion USD, GDP per capita [5000, 8000] USD, life expectancy [58, 70] years, population [55, 68] million, electricity access [80, 100]%. GDP and population values must be rounded to one decimal place.
